title: Senior Data Analyst
description:
Join our team and what we\'ll accomplish together

The AI Accelerator team is on a continuous journey towards helping TELUS become a world-class leader in data solutions, doing so by delivering data analytics capabilities built upon unified scalable platforms, advanced AI solutions, high-quality data, and a data-product-oriented culture while always keeping an eye on the horizon, preparing for the next big thing.

We are entrepreneurial and live by our AI Manifesto of failing fast and being outcome vs technology-driven, creating value for our customers, team members, communities, and the environment. The team takes pride in our Artificial Intelligence and Machine Learning capabilities and takes ownership of each step of the process. From hypothesis generation, initial exploring of datasets, developing novel AI techniques to discover insights, to developing automation pipelines and web visualizations, we do it all!

Always wanted to work with a team of innovators touching all business units within TELUS, and be part of a culture that embraces creativity and collaboration? If so, we’d love to talk with you!

You’ll be a part of the team and journey that will transform the way we do business across various domains. You’ll collaborate with teams across the company, seeking out various data sources to help identify new business opportunities while championing data-driven decision-making and the accelerated adoption of AI.

As a Senior Data Analyst on the team, you will combine your expert knowledge of data analytics and visualization with business acumen to create reporting tools to facilitate data exploration, analytics, to enable data-driven decision making for business stakeholders.

What you\'ll do

  • Tackle complex data analysis challenges, working with big data to uncover key insights that drive business outcomes.
  • Write SQL code involving multiple tables and aggregations, ensuring optimal query performance.
  • Design and develop robust data models that effectively organize and structure data to support complex queries, reporting, and analytics. Ensure models are scalable, maintainable, and aligned with business requirements.
  • Lead the development of visually compelling, interactive dashboards and reports using tools like Looker, DOMO, and Tableau. Transform raw data into easy-to-understand insights that directly impact decision-making. Create user-friendly data visualizations that communicate complex information effectively to a diverse audience, from executives to operational teams.
  • Engineer end-to-end data solutions primarily on GCP services like BigQuery, GCS, Cloud Composer, and Looker, ensuring seamless integration and performance.
  • Engage with various business stakeholders to gather requirements, develop dashboards and reports, and ensure the delivery of actionable insights.
  • Lead a team of developers towards the implementation of operational and partnership dashboards using Looker, to help the leadership make informed data-driven decisions

What you bring

  • 6+ years in roles such as Data Analyst, Business Intelligence Analyst, Data Engineer, or similar, with a strong focus on SQL and data modeling
  • Expertise in creating and managing interactive dashboards and reports using leading BI tools such as Looker, DOMO, and Tableau. Ability to transform data into actionable insights for business stakeholders
  • Strong experience with relational database systems, particularly BigQuery, Oracle, and SQL Server. Demonstrated ability to write complex SQL queries and optimize database performance
  • Strong ability to identify requirements and build reporting solutions in collaboration with stakeholders
  • Exceptional attention to detail, with the ability to prioritize, multi-task, and meet tight deadlines in a fast-paced environment
  • Proven track record of driving data-based decisions in a consultative style with executive and senior management stakeholders


Great-to-haves

  • Post-secondary degree in Computer Science, Engineering, or a related field
  • 5 years of experience in the telecom industry
  • Certifications in Data Engineering or Business Intelligence technologies

title: Senior Data Scientist
description:
About the Role

The Senior Data Scientist will be a key member of the data analytics team. In this role, you will use your expertise to solve data related challenges in manufacturing operations and vehicle data analysis. You have a strong sense of curiosity, along with an open mindset. Challenging the status quo, you continuously strive to learn and reach for new and innovative ideas. We encourage all individuals with an inventive mind who enjoy working with others to apply, we’d love to hear from you.

Your Responsibilities

· Lead the data projects, apply, and inspire the adoption of advanced data science, analytics, and computer vision across the team and projects.

· Develop strategy and applications of statistical analysis, Machine Learning, Computer vision, and AI tools to improve product development, manufacturing, predictive maintenance, and business processes.

· Support, plan, and execute the initial and wider deployment and adoption of ML and AI initiatives, PoC, and small-scale implementation.

· Conceptualize Machine Learning problems, apply appropriate theory, explore approaches, execute simulations, and implement with minimal supervision.

· Leverage internal and external expertise as required.

· Regularly interface with senior management to provide project updates.

· Occasionally responsible to hire, lead, supervise, and evaluate indirect hires, such as summer students.

Who we are looking for

· Master’s Degree in Data Analytics, Mathematics, Statistics, Computer Science, or a closely related education with 7+ years of experience in industry with a focus on data analytics and/or computer vision.

· Alternate: Bachelor's degree in Mathematics, Statistics, Computer Science, or a closely related education with 10+ years of experience in industry with a focus on data analytics and/or computer vision.

· PhD preferred

Your preferred qualifications

· Experience with a wide variety of ML and AI techniques and algorithms

· Experience with computer vision, structured light based 3D reconstruction of point cloud or mesh, feature estimation

· Experience with optimization algorithms for processing time and memory, parallel processing and implementation / Cuda

· Knowledge of C++

· Experience with agile project management and related tools such as Confluence and JIRA

· Experience with cloud-based tools by Microsoft, AWS, Databricks

· Analytic tools and languages for statistical and machine learning models, especially Python

· Deep Learning frameworks and tools such as TensorFlow

· Data management using PySpark and SQL

· DevOps – GIT, Docker, CI/CD

Accommodations for disabilities in relation to the job selection process are available upon request.

Candidates will be required to complete a Criminal Records Check and, if deemed necessary, a Credit Check as part of the candidate selection process.

What we offer

At Magna, you can expect an engaging and dynamic environment where you can help to develop industry-leading automotive technologies. We invest in our employees, providing them with the support and resources they need to succeed. As a member of our global team, you can expect exciting, varied responsibilities as well as a wide range of development prospects. Because we believe that your career path should be as unique as you are.

Site Benefits

Awareness. Unity. Empowerment.

At Magna, we believe that a diverse workforce is critical to our success. That’s why we are proud to be an equal opportunity employer. We hire on the basis of experience and qualifications, and in consideration of job requirements, regardless of, in particular, color, ancestry, religion, gender, origin, sexual orientation, age, citizenship, marital status, disability or gender identity. Magna takes the privacy of your personal information seriously. We discourage you from sending applications via email to comply with GDPR requirements and your local Data Privacy Law.

title: Director, Senior Data Analyst, Office of Chief Investment Officer - SLC Management
description:
SLC Management is a growing global asset management firm providing innovative and diversified investment solutions to institutional investors. We are breaking new ground with a full spectrum of solutions for a growing institutional client base of more than 1300 organizations. Our history of organic and strategic growth reflects this commitment with over CA$374/US$283 billion in assets under management (AUM). As a subsidiary of Sun Life, we are aligned with one of the world’s leading financial services companies, and benefit from the stability and strength of that relationship.

We are a performance-driven organization with a deliberate focus on fostering a development-focused, inclusive culture revolving around our Core Values of being client obsessed, valuing each other, acting with speed and having an owner’s mindset. As part of our team, you play a role in fulfilling our purpose and making a difference. We offer a rewarding work environment that is filled with opportunity for growth where you will be empowered to meet the challenges of a highly competitive landscape.

Visit our website to learn more and for the most up to date AUM information.

SLC Management offers a mix of in-office and virtual work where our employees are empowered to do their best. Generally, employees are expected to work three or more days in-office each week with flexibility depending on their business, client and team needs.

Job Description:

Director, Senior Data Analyst, Office of the Chief Investment Officer (CIO) – SLC Management

WHAT IS IN IT FOR YOU

The Director, Senior Data Analyst will work in partnership with the Office of the CIO, and the SLC Digital Solutions & Innovations team to identify areas of opportunities where data analysis (including statistical modeling, AI, etc) can be leveraged to create a positive outcome for the asset management business.

Reporting to the Managing Director, Chief of Staff, Office of the CIO & the Senior Managing Director, Client Experience, Data & Analytics, and Innovation Platforms Owner, the Senior Data Analyst will leverage their in-depth asset management and/or capital markets knowledge to provide a hands-on, strong analytical and data-driven approach to help organize, visualize, and understand large data sets. 

We are looking for an individual who thrives in a fast-paced and agile environment and knows how to juggle multiple deadlines. We are looking for a clear communicator who is comfortable negotiating and making recommendations to various levels of leadership. 

WHAT YOU WILL DO

  • Define and clearly articulate problem statements to allow for collaboration on solutions that meet business needs.
  • Facilitate sessions and drive discussions to uncover critical and/or hidden requirements, the true business need, and associated risks.
  • Leverage the data assets to provide recommendations and convey key insights to answer business questions. Communicate data insights and findings across the organization via presentations & documents.
  • Create re-usable data assets for the firm by acquiring, digesting, and analysing raw datasets.
  • Create unique and descriptive visualizations that bring data to life, intuitively telling the story behind data.
  • Work with engineering to develop scalable data pipelines that allow for accurate and insightful views into the data.  
  • Manage discussions, conflicts, and negotiations through to resolution, using communication strategies that align with different audience styles, environments and/or cultures.
  • Work in collaboration with teams across SLC Management and SLF Corporate

WHAT YOU WILL NEED TO SUCCEED:

  • 7+ years’ experience in a data analyst role, ideally from asset management industry (CFA preferred).
  • Expert at writing SQL queries and data analysis with python.
  • Extensive experience creating visualization using various tools such as PowerBI, and Tableau.
  • Expert at pulling, cleaning, and manipulating large sets of data into insights.

Preferred Qualifications:

  • Have the ability to work independently with your business partners to deliver an outcome.
  • Understanding of common statistical methods and their real-world applications.
  • Experience operating in an ambiguous environment with competing priorities.

Why SLC Management?

  • Opportunity to work for a growing global institutional asset manager
  • Excellent benefits and wellness programs to support the three pillars of your well-being – mental, physical and financial – including generous vacation and sick time, market-leading paid family, parental and adoption leave, a partially-paid sabbatical program, medical plans, company paid life and AD&D insurance as well as disability programs and more
  • Retirement and Stock Purchase programs to help build and enhance your future financial security including a 401(k) plan with an employer-paid match as well as an employer-funded retirement account
  • A flexible work environment with a friendly, caring, collaborative and inclusive culture focused on career growth development #WeWinAsATeam
  • The opportunity to move along a variety of career paths with amazing networking potential
  • Award winning workplace culture - Great Place to Work® Certified in Canada and the U.S., “Best Places to Work in Money Management” by Pension & Investments, “Top 10” employer by the Boston Globe\'s “Top Places to Work” two years running

SLC Management is a regulated business, and this means that there are certain financial reporting and trading preclearance requirements for all employees. title: Risk Specialist Analyst
description:

As part of the role, the analyst will provide maned services of vulnerability patch management in Wealth Management. As part of this role, you know Power BI, understand the development life cycle and be able to communicate vulnerabilities with the Senior Leadership team.

Supports the execution of accurate and efficient reporting solutions for risk, regulatory and management information to internal and external stakeholders including regulatory bodies. title: Associate Director, Data Insights
description:
Job Summary

Job Description

What is the opportunity?

In the increasingly complex world of financial services, accurate and complete data is critical for risk management and regulatory compliance. Ensuring data integrity begins with an understanding of financial services data, data quality controls and identifying and resolving data observations and issues. As an Associate Director, Data Insights, you will be accountable for driving improvements in Risk and Finance data through the management of identification, root cause analysis, and resolution of data issues for US business critical processes and regulatory reports. Reporting to the Director, you will be responsible in building trust and ensuring the accuracy and completeness of shared data assets and reporting data by managing data quality initiatives.

What will you do?

Data Quality monitoring, observation identification and management

  • Review and assessment of exceptions and observations management investigations and reporting, and overseeing and monitoring the remediation of data and processes, as required.
  • Managing a team responsible for identifying and investigating data quality observations and issues, performing root cause analysis, and determining steps required to remediate the data and/or business processes.

Investigations and reporting

  • Manages and conducts detailed investigations and report findings based on data exceptions, identified observations, and ad hoc requests from critical Report owners and other users of shared data assets.
  • Manage a team of analysts that provides monthly summary and detailed data quality reporting to business partners, stakeholders and executive leadership, including data quality exceptions, findings and the status of outstanding observations.

Continuous improvement

  • Regularly review and update data quality process documentation for accuracy and completeness, monitor their efficiency and effectiveness and recommend/implement improvements to controls, investigations, reporting, observations management and work flows.
  • Based on root-cause analysis findings, propose process changes/improvements to address gaps or inconsistencies in data, processes and/or controls and raise awareness of best practices and approved provisioning points for commonly used data assets.
  • Maintain data quality tests and data quality observation repositories

Leadership and collaboration

  • Provide leadership and communicate effectively with team members, stakeholders and business partners, resolving conflicts/issues of medium complexity and escalating to others as appropriate.

What do you need to succeed?

Must-have

  • 7 + years of experience in financial services and systems including finance and credit risk data, data quality principles, data management, business analysis.
  • Knowledge of financial reporting and credit risk business concepts and procedures.
  • Intermediate to advanced knowledge of data analysis methods and the ability to analyze and resolve complex data related issues.
  • Experience conducting root cause analysis, using tools such as SQL, Excel.
  • Experience with business and/or technical documentation and the assessment of existing processes and systems.

Nice-to-have

  • Ability to manage multiple competing priorities in times of stress.
  • Experience working with financial services and systems including finance and/or credit risk data
  • Experience using Collibra or a similar data management or workflow platform

What’s in it for you?

We thrive on the challenge to be our best, progressive thinking to keep growing, and working together to deliver trusted advice to help our clients thrive and communities prosper. We care about each other, reaching our potential, making a difference to our communities, and achieving success that is mutual.

  • A comprehensive Total Rewards Program including bonuses and flexible benefits and competitive compensation
  • Leaders who support your development through coaching and managing opportunities
  • Work in a dynamic, collaborative, progressive, and high-performing team
  • Opportunities to do challenging work

#LI-PK

Accountability, Business, Communication, Critical Thinking, Data Analysis, Data Governance, Data Integrity, Data Management, Data Movement, Data Quality, Data Warehousing (DW), Information Capture, Knowledge Organization, Leadership, Management Reporting, Microsoft Excel, People Management, Repository Tools, Structured Query Language (SQL)

Minimum qualifications:

About the job

Join us for a full-time, 12-14 week paid internship that offers personal and professional development, an executive speaker series, and community-building. Research happens across Google everyday, in many different teams. Our research has already impacted user-facing services across Google, including Search, Maps, and Google Now, and is central to the success of Google Cloud and our planet-scale computing, storage, and networking infrastructure.

Research Interns work closely with Developers to discover, invent, and build at the largest scale. Ideas may come from internal projects. From creating experiments and prototyping implementations to designing architectures, Research Interns work on challenges in artificial intelligence, machine perception, data mining, machine learning, natural language understanding, privacy, computer architecture, networking, operating systems, storage and data management, and more.

Google Research addresses challenges that define the technology of today and tomorrow. From conducting fundamental research to influencing product development, our research teams have the opportunity to impact technology used by billions of people every day.

Our teams aspire to make discoveries that impact everyone, and core to our approach is sharing our research and tools to fuel progress in the field -- we publish regularly in academic journals, release projects as open source, and apply research to Google products.

title: Senior Business Analyst, Data Integration & Performance Platform
description:
Choose a workplace that empowers your impact.

Join a global workplace where employees thrive. One that embraces diversity of thought, expertise and experience. A place where you can personalize your employee journey to be — and deliver — your best.

We are a purpose-driven, dynamic and sustainable pension plan. An industry leading global investor with teams in Toronto to London, New York, Singapore, Sydney and other major cities across North America and Europe. We embody the values of our 600,000+ members, placing their best interests at the heart of everything we do.

Join us to accelerate your growth & development, prioritize wellness, build connections, support the communities we live and work.

Don’t just work anywhere — come build tomorrow together with us.

Know someone at OMERS or Oxford Properties? Great! If you\'re referred, have them submit your name through Workday first. Then, watch for a unique link in your email to apply.

This is an opportunity to join a forward-looking team to progress a digital transformation that will enable OMERS Capital Markets businesses and supporting functions to deliver on our pension promise. Our team is at the early stages of defining a strategic roadmap, and the selected candidate will work with internal partners to understand their needs and deliver outcomes, with excellence. This role joins a team on a journey, with significant future potential in a global, sophisticated, and complex enterprise.

We believe that time together in the office is important for OMERS and Oxford, the strength of our employees, and the work we do for our pension members. Our flexible hybrid work guideline requires teams to come to the office a minimum of 3+ days per week.

Scope of Position

Reporting to the Director, Data Integration & Performance Platform, the Senior Business Analyst will support and deliver a variety of change initiatives to mission critical data and investment performance platforms. The incumbent will ensure that platforms align to department objectives, and the needs of our internal partners by delivering high value solutions and resolving issues.

Responsibilities

  • Lead and participate in both Agile and Waterfall projects related to data and performance platform enhancements, system integrations, and process improvements that benefit OMERS investment teams, Corporate Finance and Capital Markets Finance

  • Ensure that project scope, objectives, deliverables, and timelines are clearly documented and communicated

  • Collaborate with cross-functional teams to gather and analyze requirements for projects impacting the OMERS investment data warehouse, and performance measurement platform (e.g. user stories, business requirements documents, BPMN, data flow diagrams, etc.)

  • Design and develop complex investment reporting (SQL, SSRS, OLAP, Power BI etc.)

  • Define and execute business test plans, including performance and integration tests

  • Ensure change management principles are considered throughout delivery, including the early capture of stakeholder impacts, end user training, and communications

  • Partner with technology teams to implement system enhancements, upgrades, and patches

  • Act as a subject matter expert for the data warehouse and performance measurement platforms

  • Develop and maintain support documentation, including troubleshooting guides and knowledge base articles

  • Ensure data accuracy, integrity and availability by monitoring and resolving production data and platform issues, ensuring minimal disruption to investment teams

  • Develop and maintain data documentation, including data dictionaries, data lineage, and metadata

  • Develop relationships among partners by supporting a culture of transparency, collaboration, and service excellence

Qualifications

  • Minimum 6 years experience in business analysis roles supporting data warehouse platforms, including production support, change initiatives and/or large transformation programs

  • Expert knowledge of business analysis strategies (requirements elicitation, business process modeling, user stories, source to target mapping etc.)

  • Expert knowledge of SQL and relational databases

  • Expert knowledge of ETL/ELT tools and design patterns

  • Knowledge of investment performance measurement and attribution methodologies for fixed income and equity strategies

title: Senior Data Analyst (GCS)
description:
Job Summary

Job Description

What is the opportunity?

At RBC we stand at the forefront of safeguarding RBC assets across the Public Cloud landscape. Our mandate is crystal clear: to ensure the epitome of security for critical digital infrastructure while navigating the ever-expanding cloud ecosystem. We are not just about defense; we are trailblazers in data-driven security, harnessing the immense power of technological innovation to stay ahead of potential threats.

With our sophisticated tech stack—comprising of industry-leading tools like Databricks, Python, PySpark, and Tableau—we don\'t just analyze data; we transform it into a strategic asset. Our commitment goes beyond traditional security measures as we scrutinize complex datasets and distill them into actionable business insights, thereby fortifying our cyber resilience.

Collaboration is the cornerstone of our success. We empower our team members to bring their narrative prowess and technical skills to the table, creating an environment where innovation thrives and the next breakthrough in cloud security is always on the horizon.

What you will do?

  • Design and maintain robust data models enabling efficient analysis and reporting on Databricks.
  • Expertly construct and execute complex SQL queries for data analysis and insight generation.
  • Utilize Python and PySpark to manipulate and analyse large datasets in a cloud environment.
  • Develop rich storytelling visuals and dashboards in Tableau, translating data into business narratives.
  • Communicate intricate analytics concepts to stakeholders in an intuitive and meaningful way.
  • Lead projects and collaborations with cross-functional teams, providing data-centric strategies for public cloud security.
  • Synthesise and present detailed findings to executive-level management through effective story-driven reporting.
  • Mentor junior team members and help foster a culture of data literacy and excellence within the company.
  • Stay abreast of advancements in data analytics, data modeling, and visualisation to innovate on our data practices.

What do you need succeed?

Must Have:

  • 5+ years of experience in a data analyst, data scientist, or similar role, with an emphasis on SQL, data modeling, and narrative-driven data presentation.
  • Solid experience working with popular Python libraries such as Pandas, NumPy, and notebook environments
  • Advanced SQL abilities and experience querying complex datasets, preferably in a Databricks environment.
  • Extensive experience with Databricks solutions PySpark Spark SQL and cloud data platforms preferably Azure Cloud
  • Excellent problem-solving skills and ability to troubleshoot complex data issues
  • Expertise in Tableau for crafting insightful and engaging data visualizations.
  • Exceptional storytelling skills, with a track record of translating complex data into compelling business insights.
  • Bachelor\'s or Master\'s degree in Computer Science, Data Science, Statistics, Information Systems, or a related field. A higher degree or certifications related to data analysis is a plus.
  • Outstanding interpersonal and leadership skills, with the ability to mentor and lead teams.
  • A portfolio showcasing your analytical, modeling, and storytelling expertise (links or attachments required with application).

Nice to Have:

  • Experience working on an Agile team
  • Familiarity with Cyber Security Concepts
  • Certification in Tableau and/or relational databases

What’s in it for you?

We thrive on the challenge to be our best, progressive thinking to keep growing, and working together to deliver trusted advice to help our clients thrive and communities prosper. We care about each other, reaching our potential, making a difference to our communities, and achieving success that is mutual.

  • A comprehensive Total Rewards Program including bonuses and flexible benefits, competitive compensation, commissions, and stock where applicable
  • Leaders who support your development through coaching and managing opportunities
  • Ability to make a difference and lasting impact
  • Work in a dynamic, collaborative, progressive, and high-performing team
  • Flexible work/life balance options
  • Opportunities to do challenging work
  • Opportunity to move into Data Scientist and Data Engineering roles.

title: Manager, Advanced Analytics & Controls, International Provisions
description:
Requisition ID: 209211

Join a purpose driven winning team, committed to results, in an inclusive and high-performing culture.

The Manager will contribute to the overall success of the Retail Credit Provisions in International Banking ensuring specific individual goals, plans, initiatives are executed / delivered in support of the team’s business strategies and objectives. They will ensure all activities conducted are in compliance with governing regulations, internal policies and procedures.

Is this role right for you? In this role, you will:

  • Champion a customer focused culture to deepen client relationships and leverage broader Bank relationships, systems and knowledge.
  • Assist in the design the divisional credit provisioning analytics framework and portfolio evaluation process, ensuring ongoing adequacy of the international credit reserves is supported by advanced analytics. Exercises judgment while employing various data techniques and procedures to obtain insights.
  • Assist in the design, develop and implement financial risk management provision forecast reports across international portfolios and with end-to-end workflow solution along with ETL process, database solutions, program coding and data visualization tools. Gathers information across various sources to complement reserve forecasting and manages end to end design, development, maintenance and management of database provisions details.
  • Contribute in the consolidation and aggregation of International Retail Provisions metrics for both Actuals, Plan and Forecast metrics, establishing controls that are aligned with the Bank’s end user control (EUC) directive.
  • Assist in the provisioning methodology (IFRS9) by validating information, running models, and ensuring the results obtained are as expected.
  • Ensure accuracy, integrity, validity and completeness of the information and models used for provision assessment under the IFRS 9 methodology.
  • Contributes to the internal and external audit process, including SOX controls and compliance with external regulations including OSFI and IFRS.

Do you have the skills that will enable you to succeed? We’d love to work with you if you have:

  • Previous experience in Credit Risk analytics such as predictive analytics and data mining.
  • Prior loan loss provision and financial modelling would be an asset.
  • Integrity, high attention to detail, thoroughness and accuracy are critical to ensure quality outputs.
  • Good interpersonal and communication skills are essential in dealing with many senior internal and external stakeholders, as well as preparing reports for Executive Management.
  • Tools commonly used (intermediary): Power BI, Google Cloud, SQL and Python
  • Solid organizational and time management skills to manage competing activities within short time frames.
  • An undergraduate degree in a quantitative subject (Economics/ Accounting/ Finance/STEM discipline). With preferred post graduate qualifications (CFA, CPA, FRM etc.)

What’s in it for you?

  • An inclusive & collaborative working environment that encourages creativity, curiosity, and celebrates success!
  • We offer a competitive rewards package: Performance bonus, Employee Share Ownership Program, and Pension Plan Matching, Health Benefits from day one!
  • Your career matters! You will have access to career development and progression opportunities.

title: 2025 Internship Program- Data Analytics & AI
description:
​Req Id: 420056


At Bell, our purpose is to advance how Canadians connect with each other and the world. We achieve this by providing consumers and businesses with the best network technologies in the world, innovative digital solutions and seamless customer experiences. It’s all developed and delivered by the members of #TeamBell and we’re always on the lookout for people with great skills and experiences.

We value diversity and provide a supportive, inclusive community where all team members can succeed. And through our commitment to environmental, social and governance initiatives, you will feel good about the greater impact you will have – making every day better for people as they connect, work, learn and play.


Join us. You belong at Bell.

Launch your career at Bell and join the best-in-class Graduate Program. You’ll be empowered to shape your future and inspired to bring your best ideas to work. Throughout the program, you’ll have the chance to connect with mentors and senior leaders who will support your professional and personal growth journey. You’ll develop real-world skills through Bell U, our exclusive learning platform and join other grads attending exclusive events and networking opportunities.

At Bell, you'll have the opportunity to make a meaningful difference, using your most innovative ideas to bring the best digital connections and experiences to Canadians.

Why join us?

  • Benefit from unique opportunities to engage in high-impact projects across our business to develop skills and instincts that will benefit your studies and future career.
  • Deliver on transformational projects to launch new product roadmaps, services, and solutions, to enable the customer to do more.
  • Expand your professional network with industry leaders, making valuable connections for your future including one-to-one mentorship with experienced leaders.
  • Develop your analytical, project management, and communication skills with training and hands-on, real-world experiences to challenge and grow you.
  • Interact with our peer-to-peer support network of #TeamBell community and recent grads enrolled in our full-time Graduate program.
  • Develop your technical and leadership skills through formal and experiential training programs.
  • You will have the opportunity to secure a full-time permanent position in our award-winning Graduate program.

What you’ll do

As an intern, you will support the cutting-edge of Bell’s transformation in critical technologies such as Cloud, AI/ML and Data. You’ll be a part of projects that expose you to the latest software and tools, collaborating with teams across Bell for process improvement and automation. Your day-to-day experience will include opportunities to offer insights that influence business decisions and technology investments across Bell.

What we’re looking for

Within our Data Analytics & AI function, we have several opportunities available. For more details on each of the below roles, click here.

  • Data Scientist
  • Data Analyst
  • Machine Learning Engineering
  • Data Engineering

Requirements

You’re a first to third year student studying towards a university degree OR college/CEGEP diploma in computer science, mathematics, statistics, computer engineering, electrical engineering, or related fields.

Technical skills (you have some of the following)

  • You can analyze, process, and visualize complex data quickly.
  • You have experience using SQL for data exploration and insights.
  • You have knowledge of data analytics theory, including statistical analysis and quantitative and qualitative modeling.
  • You are familiar with programming languages like Python, Scala, R, or similar.
  • Some knowledge of analytical tools such as Looker, Kibana, Tableau, and Microstrategy.
  • You have knowledge of machine learning and artificial intelligence concepts and their potential applications.
  • You are aware of Agile and CI/CD methodologies in software development.

Leadership Skills

  • You are willing to challenge the status quo, experimenting to find solutions that will make a difference in the lives of Bell’s customers.
  • You are goal-oriented and can focus on individual tasks while also understanding the bigger picture and how systems work together.
  • You are a skilled communicator balancing urgency, empathy and integrity. You speak and write clearly and concisely, and you listen with the purpose of encouraging transparent dialogue.
  • You gain the trust of your teams, being known for demonstrating fairness and respect.
  • You promote belonging and are flexible, accountable and collaborative.
  • You are committed to growing your skills and learning from others.

Applying is simple

Our application process is designed to be straightforward, so you can show us who you are and we can easily spot your #TeamBell potential.

1. Review our programs and indicate up to three role choices in your application.
2. Complete your video interview within 48 hours.
3. If selected, you will be invited to complete a phone interview or assessment to better understand your skills and experience.
4. If selected from previous step, you will be invited to a virtual panel interview.

Get started by clicking Apply on this page. Submit a resume, and most recent transcripts (unofficial accepted) to be considered.

Offers will be made to qualified interns ongoing from September to December 2024 or until all positions are filled.

title: Associate, Global Analytics and Financial Engineering - Toronto
description:
Requisition ID: 210521

Join a purpose driven winning team, committed to results, in an inclusive and high-performing culture.

Global Banking and Markets (GBM) is a leading Canadian Capital Markets and Investment Banking business with a growing platform in the US and Latin America, operating globally for over 100 years. Scotiabank’s strong U.S. presence provides our clients an important bridge to this key global market for trade and investment flows across the Americas and the world.

Global Banking & Markets provides a full range of investment banking, credit and risk management products and services relevant to the financing and strategic development needs of our clients. Our products include debt and equity financing, mergers & acquisitions, corporate banking, institutional equity sales, trading and research, fixed income products, derivatives, energy, foreign exchange and precious & metals. We also cross-sell the full range of wholesale products and services offered by the Scotiabank Group.

Be part of an innovative, Global Capital Markets and Investment Banking business with a unique geographic footprint that puts capital to work for our clients across industries! We work together to drive ambition for every future!

Global Analytics and Financial Engineering is a critical function within the Bank’s Global Banking and Markets division. The modelers in the department provide the models for pricing and risk management across the business lines. The models developed by the department are used for pricing, hedging, internal and external capital, and trading limits.The associate will be focusing on projects relating to model performance monitoring, computation of initial margin and model governance. Ensures all activities conducted are in compliance with governing regulations, internal policies and procedures.


What You'll Do:

  • Ensure newly developed models or changes to existing models to meet regulatory requirements (e.g., risk sensitivities, residual risk add-ons, etc.) for FRTB capital calculations.
  • Assist in conducting and documenting ongoing perfomance monitoring of front-office pricing models.
  • Work to streamline ongoing monitoring processes where applicable
  • Assist in conducting testing and ongoing monitoring of the initial margin model implemented at the Bank
  • Assist in preparing timely responses to requests from regualtory bodies and/or auditors around model performance monitoring and initial margin computation
  • Provide subject matter expertise to internal stakeholders regarding ongoing monitoring, intial margin or other regulatory model usage
  • Champions a high performance environment and contributes to an inclusive work environment

What You'll Bring:

  • Doctorate or Master’s degree in quantitative finance, mathematics, computer science, physics, or other quantitative areas.
  • 1-3 years’ experience in a relevant area such as model validation, regulatory projects, model governance or other relevant areas
  • Demonstrable knowledge of numerical methods and computational modelling: for example, finite difference / finite element, Monte Carlo simulation, optimization, artificial intelligence.
  • Strong programming skills in C++ or Python and the willingness to learn other programming languages.
  • Knowledge of ISDA Standard Initial Margin Model (SIMM) is considered an asset.
  • Strong organizational, analytical skills and attention to detail are a must.
  • Excellent verbal and written communication and interpersonal skills
  • Ability to work collaboratively in a fast-paced environment.


Work Arrangement:
This position is currently a hybrid role, with the expectation that you will work at least 3 days a week in the office. Please note that this is subject to change based on the needs of the business.


Interested?

If your experience is closely related but doesn’t align perfectly with every qualification, we do encourage you to apply - you might be the right candidate for this or other roles at Scotiabank!

At Scotiabank, every employee is empowered to reach their fullest potential, respected for who they are and, embraced for their differences. That’s why we work to grow and diversify talent and engage employees in a performance-oriented culture.

What's in it for you?

Scotiabank wants you to be able to bring your best self to work – and life, every day. With a focus on holistic well-being, our many flexible benefit programs are designed to help support your unique family, financial, physical, mental, and social health needs.

title: Senior Analyst/Manager - US Stress Testing
description:
Application Deadline:

01/24/2025

Address:

100 King Street West

Job Family Group:

Data Analytics & Reporting

Supports the research and development of stress testing and scenario analysis methodologies and related strategies in support of the management of risks arising from changes in the market and external environment for business/group portfolios and products. Applies knowledge of analytics algorithms and technologies to develop models, methodologies and supplementary stress testing tools and infrastructures that assess the impact of a stress on risk capital and ensure risks are properly identified, understood and risk related strategies are enabled. Summarizes statistical findings, draws business conclusions, and presents actionable business recommendations used in the management of risks and related activities e.g. defining tolerance for adverse outcomes, capital modelling, assess capital and liquidity, recovery actions, portfolio and risk management strategies and tactics.

  • Fosters a culture aligned to BMO purpose, values and strategy and role models BMO values and behaviours in all that they do.
  • Ensures alignment between values and behaviour that fosters diversity and inclusion.
  • Regularly connects work to BMO’s purpose, sets inspirational goals, defines clear expected outcomes, and ensures clear accountability for follow through.
  • Builds interdependent teams that collaborate across functional and operating groups to create the highest value for all stakeholders.
  • Attracts, retains, and enables the career development of top talent.
  • Improves team performance, recognizes and rewards performance, coaches employees, supports their development, and manages poor performance.
  • Acts as a trusted advisor to assigned business/group.
  • Influences and negotiates to achieve business objectives.
  • Recommends and implements solutions based on analysis of issues and implications for the business.
  • Assists in the development of strategic plans.
  • Identifies emerging issues and trends to inform decision-making.
  • Researches existing or emerging requirements & related best practices to assist and develops recommendations for changes/enhancements.
  • Assists with the interpretation of new or changing regulations and assessing impacts to the governance frameworks.
  • Helps determine business priorities and best sequence for execution of business/group strategy.
  • Conducts independent analysis and assessment to resolve strategic issues.
  • Leads the development and maintenance of stress testing and scenario analysis methodologies and frameworks.
  • Ensures alignment between stakeholders.
  • Represents the stress testing and scenario analysis function during internal/external regulatory audits and/or examinations.
  • Coordinates the management of databases; ensures alignment and integration of data in adherence with data governance standards.
  • Builds change management plans of varying scope and type; leads or participates in a variety of change management activities including readiness assessments, planning, stakeholder management, execution, evaluation and sustainment of initiatives.
  • Leads or participates in defining the communication plan designed to positively influence or change behaviour; develops tailored messaging; and identifies appropriate distribution channels.
  • Leads and integrates the monitoring, measurement & reporting on the status of the stress testing and scenario analysis program to internal & external stakeholders.
  • May provide specialized support for other internal and external regulatory requirements.
  • Provides input into the planning and implementation of ongoing operational programs in support of the stress testing and scenario analysis framework.
  • Leads/participates in the design, implementation and management of core business/group processes.
  • Works with stakeholders to identify the business requirements, understand the distinct problems, and the expected outcomes; models and frames business scenarios which impact critical business processes and/or decisions.
  • Administers and evaluates changes to stress scenarios and / or model parameters in response to changes in the market and the external environment.
  • Identifies where corrective actions are required and escalates per guidelines; ensures corrective action is taken as necessary.
  • Works with various data owners to discover and select available data sources from internal sources and external vendors (e.g. lending system, payment system, external credit rating system) to fulfill analytical needs.
  • Consults with stakeholders to improve consistency and transparency of risk measurement, metrics and reporting.
  • Develops and maintains in-depth knowledge of business and related risk management requirements and legislative/ regulatory directives and guidance.
  • Supports the management of governance meetings (e.g. committee, management, board meetings, etc.) as required; facilitates development and approval of agendas, gathering and distribution of documentation related to agendas, and creation of meeting minutes/output.
  • Builds effective relationships with internal/external stakeholders.
  • Ensures alignment between stakeholders.
  • Analyzes data and information to provide insights and recommendations
  • Monitors and tracks performance; addresses any issues.
  • Leads/participates in the design, implementation and management of core business/group processes.
  • Focus is primarily on business/group within BMO; may have broader, enterprise-wide focus.
  • Provides specialized consulting, analytical and technical support.
  • Exercises judgment to identify, diagnose, and solve problems within given rules.
  • Works independently and regularly handles non-routine situations.
  • Broader work or accountabilities may be assigned as needed.

    Qualifications:

  • Typically between 5 - 7 years of relevant experience and post-secondary degree in related field of study or an equivalent combination of education and experience.
  • In-depth knowledge and understanding of stress testing and scenario analysis methodologies and frameworks.
  • Proficiency in statistical/numerical software.
  • In-depth experience in quantitative risk modeling, calculation of Regulatory and Economic Capital.
  • Deep knowledge and technical proficiency gained through extensive education and business experience.
  • Verbal & written communication skills - In-depth.
  • Collaboration & team skills - In-depth.
  • Analytical and problem solving skills - In-depth.
  • Influence skills - In-depth.

We’re here to help

At BMO we are driven by a shared Purpose: Boldly Grow the Good in business and life. It calls on us to create lasting, positive change for our customers, our communities and our people. By working together, innovating and pushing boundaries, we transform lives and businesses, and power economic growth around the world.

As a member of the BMO team you are valued, respected and heard, and you have more ways to grow and make an impact. We strive to help you make an impact from day one – for yourself and our customers. title: Data ops Engineer
description:
Join our team and what we\'ll accomplish together

The Data Strategy & Enablement (DSE) team is on a continuous journey to drive TELUS to become a world-class data insights driven organization, delivering world-class data analytics capabilities built upon unified scalable platforms, advanced AI tooling, high quality data, and a data-product/data platform oriented culture. Our team members are passionate, dynamic, entrepreneurial and highly engaged, driven to achieve great outcomes for our customers, our team members, our communities and our environment. We believe in continuous meaningful transformation integrating dynamic change in our professional lives. Our team members are always learning, experimenting and applying tuition value into everything we do.


We are searching for a talented candidate to join our team in the role of DataOps Engineer. Reporting to the Manager – Analytics and Business Intelligence Platforms, the successful candidate will be responsible for implementing and supporting solutions to enable business product teams to drive value by realizing Analytics and Machine Learning use cases. This role will support our evolving DataOps landscape by providing analytics capabilities built upon unified scalable platforms, advanced AI tooling, improved speed to market, and automation. This role will require the successful candidate to design, develop, and support our evolving strategy, data, and analytics tools as well as provide strong direction for our Cloud based Analytics and Business Intelligence platforms.

What you\'ll do

  • Collaborate with Business and Product teams to enable valuable Analytics and Machine Learning use cases
  • Implement DataOps and MLOps processes and automations using the latest tools to effectively operationalize Analytics and AI workflows
  • Deploy and manage front-ends and APIs to facilitate teams’ infrastructure needs for Data Analytics and Machine Learning
  • Provision infrastructure and secure, reliable data-centric services and applications in GCP for Data & Analytics users across the TELUS enterprise
  • Identify opportunities to make improvements to the deployment and support of our DataOps environment demonstrating a DevOps mindset while improving our reliability and speed to market
  • Stay up to date of new software, tools, and organizations that are having an impact on the Analytics and Machine Learning landscape

What you bring

  • You are passionate about Agile culture and DevOps practices
  • Keen interest in Data Analytics and Machine Learning, and are familiar with the Machine Learning lifecycle
  • Experience with the data offerings in Google Cloud Platform (e.g. BigQuery, Dataproc, Dataflow, Vertex AI)
  • Experience with front-end development, back-end development, and/or customizing developer portals (e.g. Backstage)
  • Experience with Source Version Control (e.g. git), GitOps tooling (e.g. FluxCD, Argo), Kubernetes, and Kustomize
  • Hands on experience in Infrastructure-as-Code (e.g. Terraform, Pulumi) and the Python programming language
  • Communicate clearly and effectively in both technical and business matters
  • Pivot between supporting the urgent needs of the business and working on strategic initiatives
  • Experience working on and supporting large, complex technical projects that involve multiple stakeholders and business units


Great-to-haves

  • Undergrad in Computer Science, Engineering, Mathematics, Science or equivalent
  • 5+ years of progressive and relevant work experience with data/analytics platforms, DevOps, machine learning, and/or Developer Portals (e.g. Backstage)
  • GCP or other relevant Professional Certification

title: Data Product Owner– Finance Data Strategy Team
description:
Roche fosters diversity, equity and inclusion, representing the communities we serve. When dealing with healthcare on a global scale, diversity is an essential ingredient to success. We believe that inclusion is key to understanding people’s varied healthcare needs. Together, we embrace individuality and share a passion for exceptional care. Join Roche, where every voice matters.

The Position

We’re advancing science to ensure everyone has access to the healthcare they need.

We are seeking a dynamic and experienced Finance Data Product Owner to support our enterprise data product team in Finance. This role will oversee the operations of core enterprise data products in our finance data analytics domain. These products serve as key enablers for decision making in the areas of global workforce, financial costs, personnel cost, sales, research and development financials, and CAPEX. The Data Product Owner will be responsible for driving operations, managing stakeholders, ensuring effective communication with end customers, and coordinating across various teams to enhance the digital system landscape.

You need to be well versed in exploration of data and analytics, and blending this with our core data technologies in our SAP transactional and business warehouse applications and Data Mesh platforms.

You will be collaborating across Finance and beyond to ensure we architect and execute in building a sustainable data foundation for the Finance organization and our business stakeholders.

This role is for a Digital for Finance team member with extensive experience and subject matter expertise in Analytics in Finance in the Pharmaceutical or similar industry, with a comprehensive understanding of key financial processes and systems, organizational needs, and with a value focused approach.

The Opportunity

  • Managing the day-to-day operations of data products, ensuring seamless functionality and continuous improvement

    Maintaining data accuracy and compliance with organizational standards

  • Define and be accountable for key performance indicators (KPIs) for finance data products to measure their effectiveness and impact

  • Applying knowledge to the strategic application within the finance organization and providing insights to enhance decision-making for the organization

  • Work closely with cross-functional teams, including Informatics, Finance colleagues, and Business stakeholders to define product requirements to enhance the solutions

    Act as the primary point of contact for stakeholders, ensuring a prioritized and impactful requirements process is followed and implemented

  • Develop a thorough understanding of the digital system landscape, including its components, integrations, and dependencies

  • Lead the coordination of technical updates and maintenance activities, ensuring minimal disruption to end users

Who you are:

  • Minimum Bachelor’s degree in Finance or related field or equivalent professional experience

  • Minimum 5-7 years of experience working with financial systems, workforce data, and related processes

  • Proven experience in financial reporting and analysis, including cost management, budgeting, planning, and forecasting

  • Strong expertise in managing data products, with an emphasis on operations and data reconciliation

  • Knowledge of complex digital systems and an ability to coordinate across multiple teams

  • Proven experience in stakeholder management, with exceptional communication and customer interaction skills

Relocation benefits are not available for this position.

Who we are

At Roche, more than 100,000 people across 100 countries are pushing back the frontiers of healthcare. Working together, we’ve become one of the world’s leading research-focused healthcare groups. Our success is built on innovation, curiosity and diversity.

Roche Pharma Canada has its office in Mississauga, Ontario and employs over 850 employees. The Mississauga facility is bright, vibrant, fosters collaboration and teamwork, and is reflective of Roche\'s truly innovative culture.

As of January 4, 2022, Roche requires all new employees who work in Canada to be fully vaccinated against COVID-19 on the date they take office. This requirement is a condition of employment at Roche that applies regardless of whether the position is on a Roche campus or remotely. If you have a valid reason for not being fully immunized, which is limited to certain specific medical reasons or other valid reasons protected by applicable human rights laws, you may request an exemption and / or adaptation measures regarding this vaccination requirement.

Roche is an Equal Opportunity Employer.

title: Senior Manager, Transaction Screening Data and Model Analytics and Reporting
description:
Requisition ID: 210730

Join a purpose driven winning team, committed to results, in an inclusive and high-performing culture.

The Transaction Screening team is a risk management function responsible for the governance of sanctions screening solutions globally, across the entire enterprise. The Senior Manager leads the team that oversees the Global Sanctions Program’s screening solutions management functions. This includes ensuring that the tuning methodology, rules governance framework, and screening standards are developed, managed, executed, and maintained in compliance with governing regulations, as well as internal bank-wide policies and procedures.

Is this role right for you? In this role, you will:

Support the sanctions screening solutions management function globally, enterprise-wide:

  • Create and/or maintain framework documents, including tuning methodology, rules governance framework, screening standards, and other documentations involving sanctions screening systems and processes
  • Develop a tracking mechanism for documentations owned by Data and Model Analytics and Reporting team, and facilitate periodic updates and approvals to the documentations
  • Develop deep understanding and subject matter expertise of sanctions screening systems, system capabilities, and relevant processes, as well as product knowledge for payments and trade finance transactions
  • Develop and/or contribute to business requirements for sanctions screening solutions for payments and trade finance transactions
  • Develop and maintain an issues log and track issues to closure for each of the sanctions screening systems
  • Liaise and provide support to the Model Validation team to ensure the success of the annual model validation efforts for sanctions screening systems
  • Liaise and provide input to the Models & Analytics team to ensure the success of the analytics/reporting and tuning efforts for sanctions screening systems
  • Facilitate the testing and management reporting on the efficacy and efficiency of the systems
  • Lead and/or provide support to the efforts of operationalizing Risk Tolerance Frameworks for sanctions screening systems and process; support ongoing tuning effort to review and opine on tuning recommendations, business rules, fuzzy logic exceptions, etc., and facilitate the documentation and approval process
  • Consider risk-based approach in decision-making process and support risk-based approach culture throughout the Global Sanctions Program
  • Lead and/or contribute to the preparation of regulatory examination or internal audit engagements in a timely manner
  • Support the management of Sanctions Risk Appetite Framework; develops a deep understanding of the Bank’s risk appetite and risk culture should be considered in day-to-day activities and decisions

People and Culture:

  • Lead and drive a customer focused culture and deepen client relationships and leverage broader Bank relationships, systems, and knowledge.
  • Build a high-performance environment and implements a people strategy that will attract, retain, develop, and motivate their team and colleagues by fostering an inclusive work environment, communicating vision/values/business strategy and managing succession and development planning for the team and colleagues.
  • Create and foster an environment in which his/her team and colleagues pursue effective and efficient operations, while ensuring the adequacy, adherence to and effectiveness of day-to-day business controls to meet obligations with respect to operational risk, regulatory compliance risk, AML/ATF risk and conduct risk, including but not limited to responsibilities under the Operational Risk Management Framework, Regulatory Compliance Risk Management Framework, AML/ATF Global Handbook and the Guidelines for Business Conduct.

Do you have the skills that will enable you to succeed? We’d love to work with you if you have experience with:
​​​​

  • Minimum bachelor’s degree in business, law, engineering, or related field.
  • Minimum 5 years in compliance or governance functions in the financial services sector.
  • In-depth knowledge of regulatory requirements with respect to sanctions.
  • Strong communication, analytical, organizational, project management, and planning skills.
  • Advanced skills in Microsoft Office suite, including Access, Excel, Project, Visio and PowerPoint.
  • Advanced and practical skills in analytical tools such as Python, SQL and VBA, and PowerBI
  • Knowledge of business, products, operations, systems, and organization for financial services sector.

What's in it for you?

  • In-depth training to prepare you for the role, as well as ongoing coaching and feedback to help you succeed!
  • You'll be part of a diverse, collaborative, innovative, and high-performing team
  • We offer a competitive rewards package:
  • Performance bonus, Employee Share Ownership Program, and Pension Plan Matching
  • Health Benefits from day one!
  • You will relish work-life balance, team events, and opportunities to participate in the community.
  • Your career matters! You will have access to career development and progression opportunities.

title: Senior Manager, Business Performance and Analytics
description:
Requisition ID: 210601

Join a purpose driven winning team, committed to results, in an inclusive and high-performing culture.

As the, Senior Manager, Performance Management & Analytics, you lead and oversee performance measurement and analysis in support of a multitude of key stakeholders and executives across the Bank. You are responsible for effective data management including timely delivery of business performance reports and analysis.


Is this role right for you? In this role, you will:


• Develop and automate, in close coordination with IB Retail and IB Analytics, monthly/quarterly management reporting, Executive dashboards, key risk indicators (KRIs) and key performance indicators (KPIs), ensuring alignment to the Operating Model Design requirements and strategic plan, providing relevant reporting/dashboards, insights and action plans to ensure portfolio health.

Seeks opportunities to enhance processes and identifies continuous improvement opportunities to streamline processes through enhancements to policies and procedures and supports the data and reporting requirements for key collateral protection program strategic initiatives and solutions in conjunction with key stakeholders.

Responsible for completing project plan deliverables in a timely manner, representating IB Insurance at regular project team meetings (contributing actively to decisions and communicating deliverables, issues and commitments to the various stakeholders)

title: Senior Manager, Credit Card Analytics
description:
Requisition ID: 211277

Join a purpose driven winning team, committed to results, in an inclusive and high-performing culture.

The Senior Manager, Credit Card Analytics responsible for leading a team of analytics professionals in conducting analyses and making fact-based recommendations across the Retail Payment & Unsecured Lending portfolios. The incumbent will deliver strategic and tactical knowledge to facilitate the execution of business strategies to enable profitable growth of Unsecured Lending customer base, increase customer engagement and optimize revenue margins for the business. This role works with multiple stakeholders and partners within and outside Scotiabank to develop optimal strategies based on excellent analytical, database marketing, people management, communication, and relationship management skills.


In this role you will:

  • Collaborate with Retail Payment & Unsecured Lending team to profitably grow customer base. Proactively provide strategic analyses that will assist the business to optimize new & existing accounts and product mix from various channels. Grow unsecured lending customer base within multiple segments of Scotiabank customers and enhance opportunity to acquire non-Scotiabank customers.
  • Lead the data science initiatives to optimize pricing and targeting strategies to drive acquisition & retention of revolving credit balances. This role works closely with Retail Payment & Unsecured Lending team, Global Risk Management, Finance, Treasury, Customer Growth team and Channel partners to recommend optimal pricing and targeting strategies to grow unsecured credit portfolio.
  • Work closely with marketing team to develop analytical and segmentation strategies that enhance unsecured credit customer engagement initiatives while optimizing budgets & revenue margins.
  • Collaborate with Retail Marketing and Customer Decisioning teams to implement and fulfill campaigns while ensuring seamless customer experience.
  • Engage in high impact technology projects to provide subject matter expertise with respect to data architecture, systems integration, and application design. Ensure integration of new advanced analytical tools into the analytical infrastructure.
  • Evaluate new campaign ideas based on business opportunities and revenue growth; provide initial sizing and develop business case. Provide go/no go recommendation by examining overall P&L impact to the bank.

  • Collaborate with Campaign Design, Products and Retail Marketing teams to support optimal acquisition and customer migration initiatives driven through centralized campaign execution engine.

  • Work closely with segmentation/modeling experts within or outside Scotiabank to create new and effective ways of targeting customers to maximize efficiency of acquisition campaign strategies.

  • Effectively manage and prioritize multiple business initiatives, projects, and ad-hoc analyses with business stakeholders and/or external partners/consultants.

  • Lead the creation of ongoing measurement of acquisition/engagement campaign strategies relative to the pro-forma/plan. Provide actionable insights and recommendations to business stakeholders on an ongoing basis to maximize business results and enable the creation of an ongoing learning loop.

  • Continuously acquire and enhance knowledge of business/product strategies, competitive practices, market insights and other policies, technical processes, and advanced analytical tools/methodologies to best provide business solutions to all key constituents

  • Identification of portfolio trends and key statistics using advanced analytics; Effective and timely communication of strategic and operational results to the business teams

  • Providing analytical support and profit insights on a regular and ad-hoc basis within Retail Canadian Banking focusing towards driving business growth and profits

  • Provide business support to all areas of Retail Payment & Unsecured Lending team including Business development, Product, Acquisition and Analytics. Work with Finance for business case development and to report results to Senior Management as well as provide outlook/projection

  • Leverage internal/external analytics tools as well current macroeconomic & industry trend to evaluate its impact on customer & profitability of the bank; keep senior management abreast with these insights and recommendation to mitigate those risks

  • Work closely with Global Risk Management team to understand intrinsic & external risk factors to the unsecured lending portfolios; develop risk management strategies to optimize risk and revenue

  • Champions a customer focused culture to deepen client relationships and leverage broader Bank relationships, systems, and knowledge

  • Understand how the Bank's risk appetite and risk culture should be considered in day-to-day activities and decisions.

  • Actively pursues effective and efficient operations of his/her respective areas, while ensuring the adequacy, adherence to and effectiveness of day-to-day business controls to meet obligations with respect to operational risk, regulatory compliance risk, AML/ATF risk and conduct risk, including but not limited to responsibilities under the Operational Risk Management Framework, Regulatory Compliance Risk Management Framework, AML/ATF Global Handbook and the Guidelines for Business Conduct.

Do you have the skills that will enable you to succeed in this role?

  • In-depth analytics experience and solid business acumen are required. Extensive marketing & campaign analytics and decision support experience is strongly preferred along with a comprehensive understanding of portfolio optimization and financial P&L levers.
  • Previous experience of leading/coaching the team is must; Proactive, self-directed, and able to work independently as well as collaboratively in a team.
  • Build and maintain strong relationships with internal stakeholders. Understand business needs and develop tailored solutions. Act as the main point of contact for business stakeholders’ inquiries and issues. Previous experience in consulting firm is an asset.
  • The incumbent must have a good knowledge of the bank’s lending products, including Credit cards & Line of Credits, and should have familiarity with the Bank’s Risk Management activities.
  • Proficiency utilizing a variety of analytical tools including Google Cloud Platform (GCP), Big Data, Python, R, SAS, SQL and Excel and a solid knowledge and understanding of advanced analytical concepts and methodologies.
  • A proven ability to extract and interpret data; transform it into meaningful and actionable business insights effectively and accurately.
  • Exceptional oral and written communication skills, with a demonstrated ability to present and translate highly technical concepts to a wide audience.
  • A strong sense of urgency and a proven ability to understand, and effectively assess multiple, ongoing requests.
  • Strong prioritizing, planning, analytical, presentation, project management, people management and relationship building skills
  • Proven track record of success in maintaining, leading and motivating a productive team of analytics professionals.
  • Displays a high level of analytical maturity and sophistication, financial acumen and a solid understanding of profit drivers, KPIs and other factors that will contribute to successful growth initiatives.
  • Minimum Bachelor's Degree in Computer Science, Finance, Economics, Business Administration, Engineering or Statistics
  • MBA (Finance or Marketing)/CFA/FRM or Master in Analytics - preferred

What's in it for you?

  • Diversity, Equity, Inclusion & Allyship - We strive to create an inclusive culture where every employee is empowered to reach their fullest potential, respected for who they are, and are embraced through bias-free practices and inclusive values across Scotiabank. We embrace diversity and provide opportunities for all employee to learn, grow & participate through our various Employee Resource Groups (ERGs) that span across diverse gender identities, ethnicity, race, age, ability & veterans.
  • Accessibility and Workplace Accommodations - We value the unique skills and experiences each individual brings to the Bank and are committed to creating and maintaining an inclusive and accessible environment for everyone. Scotiabank continues to locate, remove, and prevent barriers so that we can build a diverse and inclusive environment while meeting accessibility requirements.
  • Upskilling through online courses, cross-functional development opportunities, and tuition assistance.
  • Competitive Rewards program including bonus, flexible vacation, personal, sick days, and benefits will start on day one.
  • Community Engagement - no matter where you choose to work from; we offer opportunities for community engagement & belonging with our various programs such as hackathons, contests, cooking with friends, Humans of Digital and much more!
